title: Ground-truth label
description:
One labelled timeline point in a scenario's `ground_truth.jsonl`. Each non-meta line of that file validates against this schema. The validator (Phase 5) compares emitted state transitions to these labels within `tolerance_ms`; the synthesizer (Phase 3) treats them as the supervised signal.

The first line of ground_truth.jsonl MAY be a meta object with shape `{schema_version, agent, scenario, recording_started_at}` (validated against the meta schema below). Subsequent lines are label objects.

ts_offset_ms.description: Offset in milliseconds from the recording's start_ts (recording-meta.json `started_at`). Labels are ordered by this field.

marker.description: Human-readable identifier for this sync point. Drivers emit `gt:<marker>` lines into a sidecar; the labeler converts them into these entries. Common markers: `driver_send_prompt_1`, `driver_observe_turn_done_1`, `driver_interrupt_1`, `driver_resume_1`, `stable_for:5000ms`.

expected_state.description: The session state the validator should observe at ts_offset_ms ± tolerance_ms.

tolerance_ms.description: Allowed deviation from ts_offset_ms when matching an emitted transition. Defaults to 1000ms when omitted.

evidence_kind.description: Which signal source the maintainer expects to evidence this transition. Synthesis's greedy compositor must produce a rule that fires on this kind. `driver_emitted` covers driver-injected sync points (e.g. send-prompt) that aren't agent-observable but anchor the timeline.
